verdicts.title: date() output in revisions list not XSS-exploitable
verdicts.analysis: The taint analyzer flagged `echo date('j F Y @ H:i:s', $time + get_option('gmt_offset') * 60 * 60)` because it treats get_option() as a taint source. However, `$time` here is the integer array key from the CSS revisions array stored in WordPress options — it is always a Unix timestamp integer, not a user-supplied string. The PHP date() function with the format 'j F Y @ H:i:s' produces output consisting only of digits, English month names, spaces, '@', and ':'. None of these characters are HTML special characters (<, >, ', ", &), so XSS via this output is impossible regardless of the input value. Furthermore, even if an attacker could influence the stored revisions (which requires edit_theme_options capability via an authenticated request with nonce), the format string constrains the output to safe characters.
verdicts.notes: The admin AJAX handler admin_action_get_revisions_list() that invokes custom_css_revisions_list() requires a valid nonce (get_revisions_list) and is only accessible to logged-in users. The page.php template path also requires edit_theme_options capability to reach.

verdicts.title: printf with unescaped $revision_date not XSS-exploitable
verdicts.analysis: The taint analyzer flagged `printf(esc_html__('Editing revision dated %s. Click %sRevert to this revision%s to keep using it.', 'so-css'), $revision_date, '', '')` because `$revision_date` is passed as an unescaped argument to printf. While it is true that esc_html__() only escapes the translation wrapper string and not the %s substitution arguments, the value of $revision_date is derived from `date('j F Y @ H:i:s', $current_revision + get_option('gmt_offset') * 60 * 60)` where `$current_revision` is obtained via `filter_input(INPUT_GET, 'time', FILTER_VALIDATE_INT)`. The FILTER_VALIDATE_INT filter ensures $current_revision is strictly an integer. The date() format 'j F Y @ H:i:s' produces only digits, English month names, spaces, '@', and ':' — none are HTML special characters. XSS is structurally impossible here.
verdicts.notes: The display_admin_page() function that sets $current_revision is only reachable from the admin menu page registered with edit_theme_options capability. The underlying concern about printf + unescaped %s arguments is a valid code quality observation, but the specific data flowing through this path is fully safe due to the integer constraint and date() format string.

ajax_handlers.verdict: secure — returns CSS stored in options/postmeta, no injection surface
secure — date() output only, see flow so-css-xss-001
secure — sanitize_css() applied before saving; capability-gated

form_save.verdict: secure — double-checked capability and nonce, sanitize_css() applied

file_operations.verdict: secure — theme slug is basename(get_template_directory()), post_id is FILTER_VALIDATE_INT; no traversal possible

preview_mode.auth: current_user_can('edit_theme_options') checked in enqueue_inspector_scripts(), inspector_templates(), inline_inspector_scripts(), disable_ngg_resource_manager()
preview_mode.verdict: secure — unauthenticated users cannot access inspector functionality

css_sanitization.verdict: removes script/style tags and comments; adequate for CSS-only content stored in options and output in <style> tags
